own_review.issue: Backward-compat boot break: at base, the raw module attribute was passed through and DeferredExtensionContext canonicalized via ModuleIdentifier.fromString, so existing configs with module="name:main" booted fine; the PR now throws XMLStreamException at parse time with no migration/auto-correct path. Same enforcement added in ExtensionAddHandler.execute breaks CLI scripts/provisioning that add /extension=name:main. Intentional per WFCORE-7116 but it is a behavior break worth flagging.
Typo in new message id 518: 'only if the is slot is not 'main'' — extra 'is'; should be 'only if the slot is not main'.

MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR is left un-deprecated while every method in the class is @Deprecated(forRemoval=true, since=28.0.0); it calls the now-deprecated canonicalModuleIdentifier and has no replacement home (ParameterCorrector is in controller, the new util is in controller-client). Grep shows zero in-tree usages — orphaned.

Deprecation cascade: ~15 in-tree files (server deployment-module parsers JBossDeploymentStructureParser10-13, ManifestDependencyProcessor, ModuleDependency, ManifestClassPathProcessor, elytron ClassLoadingAttributeDefinitions/PermissionMapperDefinitions/DirContextDefinition, domain-http ErrorContextHandler/ConsoleMode, tests) still call the now-forRemoval methods; PR migrates none of them, producing build-wide deprecation warnings and a removal blocker for 28.0.0.

Message 518 is phrased as 'Resource /extension=%s ...' but is also thrown from ExtensionXml for an XML attribute during config parsing, where no management resource is involved — slightly misleading in the boot-failure context.
Missing newline at end of file (also in ModuleIdentifierUtil.java); utility class also lacks a private constructor.

points.point: Typo 'the is slot' in message id 518
points.note: Verified in diff; minor line-ref nit (518 is the message id, file line is ~3813).

points.point: MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR orphaned, zero external references, should be deprecated or removed
points.note: Verified: grep shows only the definition; left un-deprecated in a fully forRemoval class.

points.point: Deprecation cascade: 14 external callers across server/elytron/domain-http will see warnings; migration needs controller-client dependency
points.note: Verified ~15 call-site files; accurate and actionable.

points.point: ExtensionAddHandler/ExtensionXml canonical-name enforcement will fail boot for existing configs with non-canonical names — intentional breaking change to confirm
points.note: Verified: base code canonicalized via ModuleIdentifier.fromString at load time, so 'name:main' configs previously booted; now they fail XML parsing. The single most important finding; hedged but mechanism is exactly right.

points.point: TODO on escape-sequence test cases is inherited; leaving it is fine

exceeds: Boot-time compat break for existing non-canonical configs (human only gave +1 on the related handler)
Typo in message 518 (human suggested the sentence pre-typo; v5 caught the introduced grammar error)

points.point: Typo 'the is slot' in message id 518
MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR not deprecated while all other members are; zero usages; inconsistent deprecation state
Claims old ModuleIdentifierUtilUnitTestCase.java still exists and 'now delegates to JBossModulesNameUtil via deprecated methods', testing through deprecated indirection
points.note: Fabricated: the diff git-renames the old test (similarity 54%) into controller-client; no old test class remains to delegate anything. Notably v6 had pr_files/pr_file_diff tools and still got the rename wrong.

points.point: No newline at end of JBossModulesNameUtil.java
Deprecation cascade: ~14 files still call ModuleIdentifierUtil.canonicalModuleIdentifier; need follow-up before 28.0.0 removal

missed: Boot-time backward-compat break: ExtensionXml/ExtensionAddHandler now reject previously-valid non-canonical extension names (e.g. 'name:main'), failing boot of existing configs — the highest-severity issue in the PR

qualitative: Both candidates verified the jboss-modules String overloads before endorsing the approach and both found the message-518 typo, the orphaned MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR, and the deprecation cascade. v5 clearly wins: it additionally flagged the PR's only high-severity issue — the new canonical-name enforcement breaks boot for existing configs that use 'name:main' (previously canonicalized silently via ModuleIdentifier.fromString) — which v6 missed entirely. v6 also fabricated a claim that the old ModuleIdentifierUtilUnitTestCase still exists and delegates through deprecated methods, when the diff plainly shows a git rename into controller-client; this is a presence-fabrication (not an absence-fabrication, so both counters stay 0) and is striking given v6 had pr_files/pr_file_diff tools available. Neither candidate fabricated removals or broken callers this round. Net: v5 = 5, v6 = 1.
